text:
Misato's Apartment is the building in which Misato, Pen Pen, Shinji and Asuka reside in throughout the majority of the Evangelion franchise. 

__TOC__

Profile

Misato's apartment is situated in a standard "Comfort 17" apartment block, located southeast of the main Tokyo-3 city blocks on the outskirts of the city. The apartment block appears to be occupied by others during the start of the series, however it appears to be abandoned or unoccupied in the later episodes, as only Misato's balcony has furniture and her lights are the only ones on. The apartment is where Misato is free to be her true, more relaxed self around both the Children, and guests. Before the events of episode 1, only Misato and Pen Pen lived in it, having recently moved in, and as a result of Misato's sloppiness was a complete mess. During episode 2, Shinji moves in, followed by Asuka during episode 9. The apartment appears in episodes 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 12, 15, 21, 22, 23, The End of Evangelion, and the Rebuild of Evangelion film series. Most of the non-combat scenes between Shinji, Misato and Asuka are set in the apartment. This includes some of the lighter aspects of the story like Asuka and Shinji training to "synchronize" with each other, as seen during episode 9, as well as the toothpick scene in episode 2. Some very important scenes take place in Misato's apartment, such as episode 15's kiss scene, or Kaji's phone call in episode 21. The Wall of Jericho scene in episode 9 takes place between the living room and Misato's bedroom.

The residence can be categorized as a Japanese manshon, danachi, condo, or flat, and features a relatively spacious layout of approximately 80 square meters (850 square feet). This pre-manufactured housing unit maintains a consistent layout throughout the show, centered around a central kitchen and living area.

The front entrance is a metal sliding door operated by an electronic keypad. Upon entering, you are greeted by a typical tiled genkan, with a potted plant positioned atop a short storage drawer. Following the genkan is a long hallway that leads to a storage closet and a large sliding door opening into the kitchen.

The kitchen is well-equipped with cabinets above and below the sink and stove, a hanging shelf for drying dishes, and a large refrigerator. On the northern wall, there is also a short storage compartment or table. The south wall features Pen Pen’s refrigerated room and a curtain leading to the wash facilities. Three large sliding fusuma doors separate the kitchen from the living area, which, when opened, reveal a view of the balcony. Both the living area and kitchen are equipped with air conditioning units.

The flooring in the entryway, kitchen, and Shinji’s room is paneled or varnished wood, while the living room, Asuka’s room, and Misato’s room feature a pinkish or bluish carpet, depending on the shot. Almost every room has wooden varnished baseboards and crown molding that visually match the kitchen flooring, with each door also framed in this style.

The living room is sparsely furnished, with only a TV, entertainment center, two potted plants, and two paintings. Misato’s room, located directly adjacent to the living room and separated by two sliding fusuma doors, is notably cluttered. It includes a small desk, computer, chair, a few car posters, a skateboard, and a red baseball cap. There is also a closet and additional storage space on the left side. Misato is the only resident who regularly sleeps on a Japanese futon, whereas Shinji and Asuka generally use Western-style beds. Notably, in episode 9, they are seen sleeping on futons in both the living space and Misato’s room.

The living space is used for synchronization training of Asuka and Shinji in episode 9. Asuka and Shinji’s rooms are accessed via a small hallway connected to the living space. It’s worth noting that Asuka’s room was previously Shinji’s before her arrival, and Shinji moved to a supply closet directly across the hall. Shinji’s "room" has a wooden sliding door, while Asuka’s room features the standard sliding fusuma doors. Asuka’s room appears messy in her downward spiral scenes and includes a closet on the north wall along with two windows on the south and west walls. Shinji’s room is quite minimalistic, containing only a few boxes, a bed, and either a blue or brown rug or flooring.

Although Pen Pen's refrigerated "room" has a television and lamp within it, these are not marked on the layout. Misato's closet and desk (both on the far north wall) are also not marked on the layout.

Dimensions

Based on the image provided, the square meters and size of Misato's apartment can be somewhat accurately calculated. Assuming the tatami mats in the photo are to scale (1.91 meters x 0.955 meters is standard for the region), 

Misato's Room is 2.33 meters long and 5.14 meters wide. (11.97 square meters)
Asuka's Room is 2.27 meters long and 3.60 meters wide. (8.17 square meters)
Shinji's Room is 2.20 meters long and 2.60 meters wide. (5.72 square meters)
The Living Room is 3.95 meters long and 4.56 meters wide. (18 square meters)
The Kitchen is 2.43 meters tall and 4.80 meters wide. (11.7 square meters)
The Bathroom is 1.55 meters long and 1.80 meters wide. (2.8 square meters)
The Washroom is 1.55 meters long and 1.06 meters wide. (1.6 square meters)
The W.C. is 1.60 meters long and 0.76 meters wide. (1.2 square meters)
The Entryway is 4.67 meters long and 0.82 meters wide. (3.8 square meters)
The Veranda is 9.47 meters long and 1.50 meters wide. (14.2 square meters)
Pen Pen's room is 0.53 meters long and 0.82 meters wide. (0.43 square meters)
In total, ~ 79.59 square meters (856.7 square feet)
